description: The Springfield faction of the Genovese crime family is a group of Italian-American mobsters controlling organized crime in Springfield, Massachusetts. It was founded in the 1920s and is led by a caporegime. The faction engages in racketeering, illegal gambling, loansharking, and extortion. It has been active since the 1920s and remains operational.

country_links.evidence_quote: On May 16, 2011, it was announced that Fusco had been extradited from Italy to the United States...
country_links.context: Emilio Fusco was extradited from Italy to the United States for his role in the murder of Adolfo Bruno.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Springfield faction of the Genovese crime family is a group of Italian-American mobsters who control organized crime activities in the Springfield, Massachusetts area.
country_links.context: The Springfield faction operates in Massachusetts and Connecticut, with activities including racketeering, gambling, and murder.
